input.background: The number of X-ray machine failures per week in a laboratory follows a Poisson distribution with parameter λ=2. The laboratory can repair up to two machines per week, and any failures beyond this will require repair by an outside specialist contractor.
input.question: What is the probability of having X-ray machines repaired by a specialist contractor on any given week?

output.answer:
The probability of having X-ray machines repaired by a specialist contractor is

$$
P(X \geq 3)=1-P(X \leq 2)
$$
$$
=1-\sum_{k=0}^2 \frac{2^k e^{-2}}{k!}=0.3232
$$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
